api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.html.title: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.html.summary: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge Classes AcpiMcfgNative Architecture-neutral ACPI MCFG import (PCI ECAM discovery). MADT lives in Cosmos.Kernel.Core.X64/Bridge/Import/AcpiMadtNative.cs; GIC info lives in Cosmos.Kernel.Core.ARM64/Bridge/Import/Gic/AcpiGicNative.cs. AcpiPmNative ContextSwitchNative Native imports for the low-level context switch primitives. Symbols are unified across architectures: Cosmos.Kernel.Native.X64 (Interrupts.s) and Cosmos.Kernel.Native.ARM64 (ContextSwitch.s) both export the same names, so no arch gating is needed here. CpuNative EhFrameNative Native imports for the linker-provided .eh_frame section bounds. get_eh_frame_start / get_eh_frame_end are exported by the C runtime (kmain.c) and resolve to the linker symbols on both architectures, so no arch gating is needed here. HeapNative Bridge functions that let C library code allocate from the Cosmos heap. IrqNative Native entry point invoked from the architecture-specific IRQ assembly stubs. Forwards straight into Dispatch(ref IRQContext) тАФ no indirection. KnobsNative Native import for the runtime-provided knob values table. Consumed by the AppContext plug. LimineNative Wrapper for C code to access managed Limine data (RSDP address, HHDM offset). NOTE: This is a data accessor wrapper - C code gets managed data, then continues in C. We do NOT call C code from managed code - only provide data access. MemNative Bridge functions for memcpy / memcmp used by C library code. SerialNative Bridge functions for C library code (ACPI, libc stubs) to call C# methods. NOTE: These are NOT called from C bootstrap (kmain.c) - only from library code. C bootstrap uses pure C implementations for clean architecture. SimdNative ThreadNative Stable native entry point used as the initial RIP/PC for freshly scheduled threads. The architecture-specific context-switch assembly returns (via iretq on x64 / eret on ARM64) into EntryPointStub(nint), which then calls the scheduler's managed entry implementation. Thread-start registration lives in SchedulerManager.RegisterThreadStart.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.IInterruptCon.title: Interface IInterruptController | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.IInterruptCon.summary: Interface IInterruptController Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.Core.dll Platform-specific interrupt controller. Implementations live in Cosmos.Kernel.Core.X64 (APIC/IDT) and Cosmos.Kernel.Core.ARM64 (GIC/Exception Vectors). Each owns the arch-specific dispatch path: EOI timing, ack semantics, fatal-fault handling, and any extra handler tables (e.g. GICv3 LPIs) all live in the implementation. public interface IInterruptController Properties IsInitialized Check if the interrupt controller is initialized. bool IsInitialized { get; } Property Value bool Methods Dispatch(ref IRQContext) Dispatch an interrupt delivered by the arch's assembly stub. The implementation is responsible for acknowledging (where applicable), looking up and invoking the registered handler, signalling EOI, and handling fatal CPU exceptions. void Dispatch(ref IRQContext ctx) Parameters ctx IRQContext Initialize() Initialize the interrupt system (IDT for x64, exception vectors for ARM64). void Initialize() RouteIrq(byte, byte, bool) Route a hardware IRQ to a specific vector. void RouteIrq(byte irqNo, byte vector, bool startMasked) Parameters irqNo byte vector byte startMasked bool

api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.InternalCpu.I.title: Struct InternalCpu.InterruptScope | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.InternalCpu.I.summary: Struct InternalCpu.InterruptScope Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.Core.dll A disposable scope that disables interrupts on creation and restores the prior interrupt-enable state on dispose. Critically, this saves the full RFLAGS / DAIF on entry and restores it on exit, so nested scopes are correct: an inner scope disposing inside an outer disabled region does NOT prematurely re-enable interrupts. public ref struct InternalCpu.InterruptScope Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors InterruptScope() public InterruptScope() Methods Dispose() public void Dispose()

api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.InterruptMana.title: Class InterruptManager | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.InterruptMana.summary: Class InterruptManager Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.Core.dll Architecture-neutral interrupt registration and routing. Owns the dense 256-entry handler table and the dynamic-vector allocator used by MSI programmers. The actual dispatch path (ack/EOI/LPI/fatal-fault handling) is delegated to the platform IInterruptController implementation in Cosmos.Kernel.Core.X64 / Cosmos.Kernel.Core.ARM64. public static class InterruptManager Inheritance object InterruptManager Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties IsEnabled Whether interrupt support is enabled. Uses centralized feature flag. public static bool IsEnabled { get; } Property Value bool Methods AllocateVector(IrqDelegate) Allocates an unused interrupt vector in [0x40..0xEE], registers handler for it, and returns the vector. Used by MSI / MSI-X programmers that need a fresh vector unique to their device. Throws if the dynamic range is exhausted. public static byte AllocateVector(InterruptManager.IrqDelegate handler) Parameters handler InterruptManager.IrqDelegate Returns byte Dispatch(ref IRQContext) Called by native bridge from the arch ASM stubs. Delegates to the platform controller; all arch-specific behaviour lives there. public static void Dispatch(ref IRQContext ctx) Parameters ctx IRQContext Context structure. FreeVector(byte) Releases a vector previously returned by AllocateVector(IrqDelegate): clears its handler so the slot can be handed out again (the allocator's wrap pass picks freed slots back up). Without this, every consumer teardown would permanently leak one of the 175 dynamic slots and leave a stale delegate rooted тАФ and invokable тАФ in the table. Vectors outside the dynamic range тАФ including the platform-claimed LAPIC timer and spurious vectors above it тАФ are ignored. public static void FreeVector(byte vector) Parameters vector byte Initialize(IInterruptController) Initializes the interrupt manager with a platform-specific controller. public static void Initialize(IInterruptController controller) Parameters controller IInterruptController Platform-specific interrupt controller (X64 or ARM64). SetHandler(byte, IrqDelegate) Registers a handler for an interrupt vector. public static void SetHandler(byte vector, InterruptManager.IrqDelegate handler) Parameters vector byte Interrupt vector index. handler InterruptManager.IrqDelegate Delegate to handle the interrupt. SetIrqHandler(byte, IrqDelegate, bool) Registers a handler for a hardware IRQ and routes it through the interrupt controller. public static void SetIrqHandler(byte irqNo, InterruptManager.IrqDelegate handler, bool startMasked = false) Parameters irqNo byte IRQ index (0-15 for ISA IRQs). handler InterruptManager.IrqDelegate IRQ handler delegate. startMasked bool If true, the IRQ starts masked and must be explicitly unmasked.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.MsiRouting.ht.title: Class MsiRouting | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.MsiRouting.ht.summary: Class MsiRouting Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.Core.dll Architecture-neutral PCI MSI / MSI-X binding. Both x64 (LAPIC Fixed-mode messages) and ARM64 (GICv3 ITS GITS_TRANSLATER writes) ultimately program the same shape into a device's MSI-X table тАФ a 64-bit address plus a 32-bit data dword тАФ but the routing model differs: x64: data = IDT vector chosen by the kernel; address encodes the destination LAPIC ID. No per-device prep. ARM64 ITS: data = EventID (per-device index); address = the shared GITS_TRANSLATER doorbell. The ITS must be told the (DeviceID, EventID) тЖТ LPI mapping out of band before the device fires. This file abstracts both behind IMsiBinder: each platform initializer registers a binder, then HAL-level PCI MSI-X code calls PrepareDevice(uint, uint, uint, int) once per device and BindEntry(object?, int, IrqDelegate, uint, out ulong, out uint) per MSI-X table entry. The binder owns vector / LPI allocation and any device-specific bookkeeping (ITT allocation, MAPD, MAPTI on ARM64). PCI device identity is passed in raw (bus, slot, function) form to keep this file free of HAL/Pci dependencies тАФ Core can't reference HAL upstream. public static class MsiRouting Inheritance object MsiRouting Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties IsAvailable True once a platform has registered a binder (x64 LAPIC, ARM64 ITS, тАж). public static bool IsAvailable { get; } Property Value bool Methods BindEntry(object?, int, IrqDelegate, uint, out ulong, out uint) Bind one MSI-X table entry: allocate a vector / LPI for handler, wire any platform-specific routing, and return the (address, data) pair the device should program into the entry. public static void BindEntry(object? deviceCtx, int entryIndex, InterruptManager.IrqDelegate handler, uint targetCpu, out ulong address, out uint data) Parameters deviceCtx object entryIndex int handler InterruptManager.IrqDelegate targetCpu uint address ulong data uint PrepareDevice(uint, uint, uint, int) Per-device prep called once by MsiX.Enable after the cap is located. Returns an opaque context the platform can pass back into BindEntry(object?, int, IrqDelegate, uint, out ulong, out uint); may be null on platforms that don't need per-device state (x64). public static object? PrepareDevice(uint bus, uint slot, uint function, int entryCount) Parameters bus uint slot uint function uint entryCount int Returns object RegisterBinder(IMsiBinder) Called once by the platform interrupt-controller initializer. public static void RegisterBinder(IMsiBinder binder) Parameters binder IMsiBinder

api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.IO.EarlyGop.html.summary: Class EarlyGop Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.IO Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.Core.dll Minimal early-boot GOP text renderer. Writes directly to the Limine linear framebuffer with a hardcoded 8x8 bitmap font. No back buffer, no swap, no KernelConsole dependency. Safe to call from the very first kernel instruction as long as Limine has filled in the framebuffer response (i.e. as soon as static fields are accessible). public static class EarlyGop Inheritance object EarlyGop Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields CharH public const int CharH = 8 Field Value int CharW public const int CharW = 8 Field Value int Enabled Set to false to stop mirroring serial output to the screen. public static bool Enabled Field Value bool Methods Initialize() Initialize the early GOP renderer. Called lazily on first PutChar if not called explicitly. For round displays the usable text area is inset to fit inside the visible circle. public static void Initialize() PutChar(char) Write a single character directly to the linear framebuffer. api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.IO.html.title: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.IO | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.IO.html.summary: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.IO Classes EarlyGop Minimal early-boot GOP text renderer. Writes directly to the Limine linear framebuffer with a hardcoded 8x8 bitmap font. No back buffer, no swap, no KernelConsole dependency. Safe to call from the very first kernel instruction as long as Limine has filled in the framebuffer response (i.e. as soon as static fields are accessible). Serial Multi-architecture serial port driver. x86-64: 16550 UART via port I/O (COM1 at 0x3F8) ARM64: PL011 UART via MMIO (QEMU virt at 0x09000000) Interfaces IPortIO

GetPageCountsByType(out ulong, out ulong, out ulong, out ulong, out ulong, out ulong, out ulong, out ulong, out ulong, out ulong, out ulong) Fills out per-PageType counts by scanning the RAT. Returns zeros when the heap is not yet initialized. Used by the live-debug snapshot so the host VS Code extension can show RAT composition without pausing. public static void GetPageCountsByType(out ulong empty, out ulong gcHeap, out ulong heapSmall, out ulong heapMedium, out ulong heapLarge, out ulong unmanaged, out ulong pageDirectory, out ulong pageAllocator, out ulong smt, out ulong extension, out ulong unknown) Parameters empty ulong gcHeap ulong heapSmall ulong heapMedium ulong heapLarge ulong unmanaged ulong pageDirectory ulong pageAllocator ulong smt ulong extension ulong unknown ulong GetPagePtr(void*) Get the pointer to the start of the page containing the pointer's address public static byte* GetPagePtr(void* aPtr) Parameters aPtr void* Returns byte* GetPageType(void*) Get the page type pointed by a pointer to the RAT entry. public static PageType GetPageType(void* aPtr) Parameters aPtr void* A pointer to the page to get the type of. Returns PageType byte value. Exceptions Exception Thrown if page type is not found. InitializeHeap(byte*, ulong) Init RAT based on Limine memory map usable regions only. This follows the Cosmos OS memory structure with RAT at the END of heap. public static void InitializeHeap(byte* aStartPtr, ulong aSize) Parameters aStartPtr byte* A pointer to the start of the heap (ignored, we use memory map). aSize ulong A heap size hint (ignored, we use memory map). Exceptions Exception Thrown if:
  • No usable memory found.
  • RAM start or size is not page aligned.
VirtualToPhysical(ulong) Convert virtual address to physical address for Higher Half Kernel mapping. Subtracts the Limine HHDM offset when the address is an HHDM alias; passes through values that look already-physical. Kernel-image addresses (statics, stack, code тАФ the top-2GiB window) are higher-half but NOT HHDM aliases: subtracting the offset from one fabricates a garbage physical address, and a device DMA aimed at it corrupts whatever lives there. Those are rejected loudly instead тАФ callers that need DMA from static data must copy it to a heap buffer first. public static ulong VirtualToPhysical(ulong virtualAddress) Parameters virtualAddress ulong Virtual address to convert Returns ulong Physical address

api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Memory.PageType.h.summary: Enum PageType Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Memory Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.Core.dll PageType enum. Used to define the type of the page. Data Types from 1, special meanings from 255 down. public enum PageType : byte Fields Empty = 0 Empty page. Can also indicate invalid page. Extension = 128 Extension of pre-existing page. GCHeap = 1 GC page. A page managed by the GC, here is where managed objects are allocated. HeapLarge = 7 Large heap page. HeapMedium = 5 Medium heap page. HeapSmall = 3 Small heap page. PageAllocator = 32 PageAllocator type page. PageDirectory = 11 Page Directory page SMT = 64 Page which is part of the SMT Unmanaged = 9 User Managed page

api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.Interru.title: Class InterruptEvent | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.Interru.summary: Class InterruptEvent Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.Core.dll One-shot binary completion event with auto-reset semantics, designed to be signaled from an ISR and waited on from a normal thread. Wait() blocks the caller via BlockThread(uint, Thread) until Signal() is called. Signal() only walks internal state with the spinlock held and calls ReadyThread(uint, Thread) тАФ no allocation, no interface dispatch тАФ so it is safe from interrupt context. Modeled after Mutex but reduced to the surface drivers actually need (a producer ISR and a consumer thread). Multiple consumers can wait; Signal() wakes one. Signals are counted, not latched as a single bit: two Signal()s with two parked waiters wake both, and signals arriving while no consumer waits are consumed one per subsequent Wait(). public class InterruptEvent Inheritance object InterruptEvent Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors InterruptEvent() public InterruptEvent() Properties IsSignaled Whether the event currently has pending signals no waiter has consumed yet. Read without locking; for diagnostics only. public bool IsSignaled { get; } Property Value bool Methods Signal() Signals the event. Latches the signal so the next Wait() consumes it immediately, and wakes one parked waiter (if any). Latching is required because the woken thread re-enters Wait()'s loop and must see something durable rather than just "I'm not in the waiters list anymore". Safe to call from interrupt context. public void Signal() Wait() Blocks the calling thread until Signal() is called. Consumes the latched signal on return (auto-reset). Without a scheduler thread context (scheduler feature off, or pre-scheduler boot code) this degrades to halt-and-poll on the latch instead of blocking, so single-context kernels still get correct completion semantics. public void Wait() Wait(ulong) Bounded variant of Wait(): returns false when the wait loop exhausts maxIterations without consuming a signal. Iterations are loop passes тАФ IF-enabled latch polls on the no-context/idle path, interrupt wake-ups on the blocked path тАФ so this is a hang-breaker for lost device interrupts, not a clock. public bool Wait(ulong maxIterations) Parameters maxIterations ulong Returns bool

api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.IrqLock.title: Struct IrqLockScope | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.IrqLock.summary: Struct IrqLockScope Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.Core.dll RAII scope returned by AcquireIrqSafe(). Disables interrupts and acquires the lock on creation; on dispose, releases the lock then restores the prior interrupt state. Dispose order matters: the lock must be released before interrupts are re-enabled so an ISR firing immediately after restore never sees the lock held. public ref struct IrqLockScope Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Methods Dispose() public void Dispose()

Blocking primitives (and drivers built on them) must check this before touching GetCpuState(uint): with the scheduler feature disabled тАФ or before its library initializer runs тАФ there is exactly one execution context, so callers fall back to spin/polled paths instead of blocking. public static bool IsReady { get; } Property Value bool OnThreadExitCallback public static nint OnThreadExitCallback { get; } Property Value nint ThreadCount Returns the number of registered threads. Threads { get; } Property Value Thread[] Methods AllocateThreadId() Allocates a new unique thread ID. public static uint AllocateThreadId() Returns uint Balance(uint) public static void Balance(uint cpuId) Parameters cpuId uint BlockThread(uint, Thread) public static void BlockThread(uint cpuId, Thread thread) Parameters cpuId uint thread Thread CreateThread(uint, Thread) public static void CreateThread(uint cpuId, Thread thread) Parameters cpuId uint thread Thread ExitThread(uint, Thread) public static void ExitThread(uint cpuId, Thread thread) Parameters cpuId uint thread Thread GetAllCpuStates() public static PerCpuState[] GetAllCpuStates() Returns PerCpuState[] GetBusyCpuTimeNs() Sum of TotalRuntime across all non-idle threads, in nanoseconds. One timer tick is charged to exactly one current thread per CPU, so this sum over a wall-clock window equals total busy CPU time for that window. Lock-free: registry slots are atomic and ulong reads are atomic on x64/ARM64; worst case is observing a stale value from an in-progress tick. public static ulong GetBusyCpuTimeNs() Returns ulong GetCpuState(uint) public static PerCpuState GetCpuState(uint cpuId) Parameters cpuId uint Returns PerCpuState GetCurrentCpuId() Returns the CPU ID currently executing this code path. Single-CPU today. TODO(SMP): replace with x86_64 GS-relative per-CPU storage or ARM64 MPIDR_EL1 affinity read once application processors are brought online. public static uint GetCurrentCpuId() Returns uint GetPriority(Thread) public static long GetPriority(Thread thread) Parameters thread Thread Returns long Initialize(uint) public static void Initialize(uint cpuCount) Parameters cpuCount uint InvokeCurrentThreadStart(nint) Entry body for newly scheduled threads. Called from EntryPointStub(nint), whose address is passed as the initial RIP / PC to the context-switch assembly by whoever creates the thread (e.g. ThreadPlug). This method handles exceptions, marks the thread as exited, and halts. The scheduler will never re-pick a halted thread; the halt loop is a safety net in case the exit path ever races with a context switch. public static void InvokeCurrentThreadStart(nint parameter) Parameters parameter nint Generic parameter of the Thread Start, it is decoded based on the ThreadFlags set in the thread. OnTick(uint, ulong) public static bool OnTick(uint cpuId, ulong elapsedNs) Parameters cpuId uint elapsedNs ulong Returns bool OnTimerInterrupt(uint, nuint, ulong) Called from timer interrupt handler to process scheduling. ReadyThread(uint, Thread) public static void ReadyThread(uint cpuId, Thread thread) Parameters cpuId uint thread Thread RegisterThread(Thread) Registers a thread in the global registry. Called during thread creation. public static void RegisterThread(Thread thread) Parameters thread Thread ReschedulePendingFromIrq(uint, nuint) Runs a pending reschedule request on hardware-IRQ exit. ReadyThread sets the request when it wakes a thread (typically an ISR-side Signal()); device-IRQ exit doesn't otherwise reschedule тАФ only the timer tick does тАФ so a woken waiter would sit in the run queue for up to a full quantum. No-op when the timer path already staged a context switch for this interrupt: a second ScheduleFromInterrupt would save this frame's stack pointer into a thread whose real context lives elsewhere. public static void ReschedulePendingFromIrq(uint cpuId, nuint currentRsp) Parameters cpuId uint Current CPU ID. currentRsp nuint Current RSP (pointer to saved context on stack). Should only be called during initialization. public static void SetupIdleThread(uint cpuId, Thread idleThread) Parameters cpuId uint idleThread Thread Sleep(uint) Puts the current thread to sleep with a timeout. public static void Sleep(uint timeoutMs) Parameters timeoutMs uint Timeout in milliseconds. 0 means indefinite sleep. The thread may be woken up either by the timeout expires or when signaled. public static void Sleep(uint cpuId, Thread thread, uint timeoutMs) Parameters cpuId uint CPU ID of the thread. thread Thread Thread to sleep. timeoutMs uint Timeout in milliseconds. 0 means indefinite sleep (until signaled). api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.SpinLoc.title: Struct SpinLock | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.SpinLoc.summary: Struct SpinLock Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.Core.dll Simple spinlock for SMP synchronization. The plain Acquire() / Release() pair is only safe for locks that are never taken from interrupt context. If an ISR can acquire the same lock as mainline code (e.g. a wait/signal pair where the ISR signals), use AcquireIrqSafe(): a single CPU holding the plain lock when the ISR fires will spin forever waiting for itself. public struct SpinLock Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Properties IsLocked public readonly bool IsLocked { get; } Property Value bool Methods Acquire() public void Acquire() AcquireIrqSafe() Disable interrupts and acquire the lock as a single scope. Dispose releases the lock then restores the prior interrupt state. Use whenever the same lock can be taken from both mainline and ISR context тАФ without IRQ-safe acquisition, a single CPU holding the lock when its ISR fires will deadlock against itself. [UnscopedRef] public IrqLockScope AcquireIrqSafe() Returns IrqLockScope Release() public void Release() TryAcquire() public bool TryAcquire() Returns bool

api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.html.title: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.html.summary: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler Namespaces Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.Stride Classes ConditionVariable A condition variable primitive for coordinating scheduler threads around state changes. ContextSwitch Low-level context switch operations. Native imports live in Cosmos.Kernel.Core/Bridge/Import/ContextSwitchNative.cs. InterruptEvent One-shot binary completion event with auto-reset semantics, designed to be signaled from an ISR and waited on from a normal thread. Wait() blocks the caller via BlockThread(uint, Thread) until Signal() is called. Signal() only walks internal state with the spinlock held and calls ReadyThread(uint, Thread) тАФ no allocation, no interface dispatch тАФ so it is safe from interrupt context. Modeled after Mutex but reduced to the surface drivers actually need (a producer ISR and a consumer thread). Multiple consumers can wait; Signal() wakes one. Signals are counted, not latched as a single bit: two Signal()s with two parked waiters wake both, and signals arriving while no consumer waits are consumed one per subsequent Wait(). Monitor A simple monitor implementation for kernel threads. Mutex A simple mutex primitive for protecting critical sections in the scheduler. PerCpuState Per-CPU scheduling state. SchedulerExtensible Base class for objects that can hold scheduler-specific extension data. SchedulerManager Manages scheduler lifecycle and dispatches to current scheduler. Thread Thread Control Block for scheduling. Structs IrqLockScope RAII scope returned by AcquireIrqSafe(). Disables interrupts and acquires the lock on creation; on dispose, releases the lock then restores the prior interrupt state. Dispose order matters: the lock must be released before interrupts are re-enabled so an ISR firing immediately after restore never sees the lock held. SpinLock Simple spinlock for SMP synchronization. The plain Acquire() / Release() pair is only safe for locks that are never taken from interrupt context. If an ISR can acquire the same lock as mainline code (e.g. a wait/signal pair where the ISR signals), use AcquireIrqSafe(): a single CPU holding the plain lock when the ISR fires will spin forever waiting for itself. ThreadContext Complete thread context saved on stack during interrupt. This represents the full stack layout after IRQ stub saves all registers. RSP points to the start of this structure after save. Interfaces IScheduler Interface for pluggable scheduling algorithms. Inspired by Ekiben's EkibenScheduler trait. Enums ThreadFlags Thread flags. ThreadState Thread execution state.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.ARM64.ARM64Platfor.summary: Class ARM64PlatformInitializer Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.ARM64 Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.ARM64.dll ARM64 platform initializer - creates ARM64-specific HAL components. public class ARM64PlatformInitializer : IPlatformInitializer Inheritance object ARM64PlatformInitializer Implements IPlatformInitializer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Architecture public PlatformArchitecture Architecture { get; } Property Value PlatformArchitecture PlatformName public string PlatformName { get; } Property Value string Methods CreateCpuOps() public ICpuOps CreateCpuOps() Returns ICpuOps CreateInterruptController() Creates the platform-specific interrupt controller. public IInterruptController CreateInterruptController() Returns IInterruptController CreatePortIO() public IPortIO CreatePortIO() Returns IPortIO CreatePowerOps() public IPowerOps CreatePowerOps() Returns IPowerOps CreateTimer() Creates and initializes the platform timer device. public ITimerDevice CreateTimer() Returns ITimerDevice DelayMicroseconds(uint) Busy-waits for at least microseconds ┬╡s without depending on interrupts or the scheduler тАФ usable from phase-3 device init. x64 paces on legacy port-0x80 reads (~1 ┬╡s each); ARM64 polls the generic timer (CNTVCT/CNTFRQ). public void DelayMicroseconds(uint microseconds) Parameters microseconds uint DmaBarrier() Full data-synchronization barrier ordering prior normal-memory accesses against subsequent device MMIO accesses. DMA drivers call this between filling a descriptor/queue entry in RAM and ringing the device doorbell (and after observing a device-written flag before consuming the data it guards). ARM64 issues dsb sy; x64's total store order already provides this, so it's a no-op. public void DmaBarrier() EnsureMmioMapped(ulong) Maps a physical MMIO region so the HHDM-virtual alias is accessible with Device-memory attributes. Called by HAL device drivers (AHCI, NVMe, etc.) before touching their BARs. ARM64 installs a Device mapping in TTBR1 via DeviceMapper.EnsureMapped; x64's existing page tables already cover MMIO so it's a no-op. public void EnsureMmioMapped(ulong physBase) Parameters physBase ulong Physical base address of the MMIO region. GetCpuCount() Gets the number of CPUs detected on this platform. public uint GetCpuCount() Returns uint GetKeyboardDevices() Gets keyboard devices available on this platform. public IKeyboardDevice[] GetKeyboardDevices() Returns IKeyboardDevice[] GetMouseDevices() Gets mouse devices available on this platform. public IMouseDevice[] GetMouseDevices() Returns IMouseDevice[] GetNetworkDevice() Gets network devices available on this platform. Returns null if no network device found. public INetworkDevice? GetNetworkDevice() Returns INetworkDevice InitializeHardware() Initializes platform-specific hardware (PCI, ACPI, APIC, GIC, etc.). Called after HAL and interrupt manager are initialized. public void InitializeHardware() PreparePciMapping(ulong) Maps PCI configuration space memory before device enumeration. ARM64 maps ECAM as device memory; x64 uses port I/O (no mapping needed). public void PreparePciMapping(ulong ecamBase) Parameters ecamBase ulong Physical ECAM base address from ACPI MCFG. StartSchedulerTimer(uint) Starts the platform timer for preemptive scheduling. Called after all initialization is complete. public void StartSchedulerTimer(uint quantumMs) Parameters quantumMs uint Scheduler time quantum in milliseconds.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.ARM64.Devices.Cloc.title: Class RTC | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.ARM64.Devices.Cloc.summary: Class RTC Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.ARM64.Devices.Clock Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.ARM64.dll ARM64 Real-Time Clock implementation. Reads boot time from the PL031 RTC (ARM Primecell RTC, present on QEMU virt machine). Uses the ARM64 Generic Timer counter for sub-second elapsed time tracking. PL031 RTCDR register provides Unix time (seconds since 1970-01-01 UTC). QEMU virt machine maps PL031 at physical address 0x09010000. public class RTC : Device Inheritance object Device RTC Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties BootTimeTicks Boot time in DateTime ticks (UTC). public long BootTimeTicks { get; } Property Value long Instance Singleton instance of the RTC. public static RTC? Instance { get; } Property Value RTC IsAvailable Whether the PL031 RTC was successfully detected and read. public bool IsAvailable { get; } Property Value bool Methods GetCurrentTicks() Gets the current time as DateTime ticks (UTC). Uses boot time + elapsed Generic Timer ticks converted to 100-ns intervals. public long GetCurrentTicks() Returns long GetElapsedTicks() public long GetElapsedTicks() Returns long Initialize() Initializes the ARM64 RTC. Reads boot wall-clock time from PL031 if available, otherwise uses a fixed fallback epoch. Captures the GenericTimer counter for elapsed-time tracking. public void Initialize()

api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Cpu.Enums.EFlagsEn.title: Enum EFlagsEnum | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Cpu.Enums.EFlagsEn.summary: Enum EFlagsEnum Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Cpu.Enums Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.dll EFlags Enum. public enum EFlagsEnum : uint Fields AlignmentCheck = 262144 When set to 1, enables alignment check. AuxilliaryCarry = 16 Set when an arithmetic carry or borrow has been generated out of the four LSBs. Carry = 1 Set by arithmetic instructions, can be carry or borrow. Direction = 1024 When set to 1, strings is processed from highest address to lowest, and from lowest to highest otherwise. ID = 2097152 When set, indicate that CPUID instruction is available. InterruptEnable = 512 When set to 1, maskable hardware interrupts will be handled, and ignored otherwise. NestedTag = 16384 Set to 1 when one system task invoke another by CALL instruction. Overflow = 2048 Set to 1 if arithmetic overflow has occurred in the last operation. Parity = 4 Set by most CPU instructions if the LSB of the destination operand contain an even number of 1's. Resume = 65536 When set to 1, enables the option turn off certain exceptions while debugging. Sign = 128 Set to 1 if the last arithmetic result was positive, and reset otherwise. Trap = 256 When set to 1, permits single step operations. Virtual8086Mode = 131072 When set to 1, Virtual8086Mode is enabled. VirtualInterrupt = 524288 When set, the program will receive hardware interrupts. VirtualInterruptPending = 1048576 When set, indicate that there is deferred interrupt pending. Zero = 64 Set to 1 if an arithmetic result is zero, and reset otherwise.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.Nv.title: Class NvmeController | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.Nv.summary: Class NvmeController Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.dll One initialized NVMe controller. Owns its admin and a single IO queue pair, and the namespaces it discovered. The admin queue is polled (it only runs once at init); the I/O queue is interrupt-driven via MSI-X so callers of Read(uint, ulong, Span<byte>, ushort) / Write(uint, ulong, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ushort) can yield instead of burning a CPU on a spinloop, and up to Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.NvmeController.IoQueueDepth-1 commands can be in flight concurrently because every slot owns its own 4 KiB DMA bounce buffer. If MSI-X is unavailable (e.g. ARM64 today, or a pathological PCI device with no MSI-X capability) the controller falls back to polled completion, but in that mode commands are serialized through an internal mutex. Limitations: One IO submission and one IO completion queue (qid=1), depth 8. At most depth-1 commands are in flight (NVMe queue-full rule: a depth-N SQ holds N-1 entries, or the wrapped tail is indistinguishable from an empty queue). Single-PRP transfers тАФ every command moves at most one 4 KiB page through the slot's bounce buffer, so PRP2 is always 0. Namespaces whose LBA format exceeds one page (or carries metadata) are skipped at discovery. public class NvmeController Inheritance object NvmeController Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors NvmeController(PciDevice, int) public NvmeController(PciDevice pci, int index) Parameters pci PciDevice index int Properties Index Zero-based index of this controller in PCI discovery order. Lets a test assert which interrupt path a given QEMU profile actually exercised rather than just that I/O works. public bool IsMsiXEnabled { get; } Property Value bool Namespaces public List<NvmeNamespace> Namespaces { get; } Property Value List<NvmeNamespace> Methods Flush(uint) Flush volatile write cache for namespace nsid. public uint Flush(uint nsid) Parameters nsid uint Returns uint Initialize() Bring the controller up: reset, set up admin queues, identify controller and namespaces, create one IO queue pair, register each namespace as a BlockDevice. public void Initialize() Read(uint, ulong, Span<byte>, ushort) Read dst.Length bytes (must equal block size * (numLogicalBlocksMinusOne+1)) starting at LBA lba from namespace nsid. api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.Sa.summary: Class Sata Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.dll SATA drive port driver. The bounce buffer is a single page allocated from PageAllocator; the HBA DMAs to its physical address while ReadDataBlock16(ushort[]) / WriteDataBlock8(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) touch the kernel-virtual address. Error contract: command issue throws on failure (no free slot, device timeout, task-file error), so ReadBlock(ulong, ulong, Span<byte>) / WriteBlock(ulong, ulong, ReadOnlySpan<byte>) never silently hand back stale bounce-buffer contents. All I/O on a port is serialized by an internal lock тАФ the port owns a single bounce buffer. public class Sata : BlockDevice, IBlockDevice Inheritance object Device BlockDevice Sata Implements IBlockDevice Inherited Members BlockDevice.BlockCount BlockDevice.BlockSize BlockDevice.BuildDeviceName(string, uint) BlockDevice.BuildDeviceName(string, string, uint) BlockDevice.BuildDeviceName(string, uint, string, uint) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors Sata(PortRegisters) public Sata(PortRegisters portReg) Parameters portReg PortRegisters Fields RegularSectorSize Regular sector size (512 bytes). public const ulong RegularSectorSize = 512 Field Value ulong Properties FirmwareRev public string FirmwareRev { get; } Property Value string ModelNo public string ModelNo { get; } Property Value string Name Human-readable device name, unique per device instance (e.g. "sata0", "nvme0n1"). public override string Name { get; } Property Value string PortNumber public uint PortNumber { get; } Property Value uint SerialNo public string SerialNo { get; } Property Value string Methods Flush() Flush the device's volatile write cache so previously completed writes are durable. The transfer moves through the port's single-page bounce buffer, so count is limited to Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.Sata.MaxSectorsPerCommand. Throws on failure. public void SendSata48Command(AtaCommands command, ulong start, uint count) Parameters command AtaCommands start ulong count uint SendSataCommand(AtaCommands) Issue a non-LBA command (Identify, CacheFlushExt, тАж). api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.ht.title: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.ht.summary: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage Classes Ahci AHCI driver entry point. Scans PCI for every MassStorageController / SataController device and brings each one up independently тАФ a machine with two SATA HBAs gets two AhciController instances and all of their attached SATA drives show up in Ports. AhciController One initialized AHCI HBA. Each controller owns its ABAR (HHDM-mapped), capability snapshot, port list, and a per-controller command region allocated from PageAllocator тАФ kernel-virtual for memset, physical (via VirtualToPhysical(ulong)) for the HBA's CLB / FB / CTBA registers. The driver scans PCI for every matching SATA/AHCI controller and instantiates one AhciController per match, so a system with two HBAs gets two instances and all of their ports show up in Ports. BlockDevice Abstract base class for all block storage devices. FisRegisterH2D FIS Register Host to Device. GenericRegisters AHCI Generic Host Control Registers. HbaCommandHeader HBA Command Header structure. HbaCommandTable HBA Command Table. CFIS is the kernel-virtual base; the HBA itself addresses this table via the physical CTBA written into the owning HbaCommandHeader. HbaPrdtEntry HBA PRDT Entry. Nvme NVMe driver entry point. Scans PCI for every MassStorageController / NvmController device and brings each one up independently тАФ a machine with two M.2 SSDs gets two NvmeController instances and all their namespaces show up in Namespaces. NvmeAdminOp NVMe admin opcodes (NVM Express 1.4 ┬з5.x). NvmeCns Identify CNS values (NVM Express 1.4 ┬з5.15). NvmeController One initialized NVMe controller. Owns its admin and a single IO queue pair, and the namespaces it discovered. The admin queue is polled (it only runs once at init); the I/O queue is interrupt-driven via MSI-X so callers of Read(uint, ulong, Span<byte>, ushort) / Write(uint, ulong, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ushort) can yield instead of burning a CPU on a spinloop, and up to Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.NvmeController.IoQueueDepth-1 commands can be in flight concurrently because every slot owns its own 4 KiB DMA bounce buffer. If MSI-X is unavailable (e.g. ARM64 today, or a pathological PCI device with no MSI-X capability) the controller falls back to polled completion, but in that mode commands are serialized through an internal mutex. Limitations: One IO submission and one IO completion queue (qid=1), depth 8. At most depth-1 commands are in flight (NVMe queue-full rule: a depth-N SQ holds N-1 entries, or the wrapped tail is indistinguishable from an empty queue). Single-PRP transfers тАФ every command moves at most one 4 KiB page through the slot's bounce buffer, so PRP2 is always 0. Namespaces whose LBA format exceeds one page (or carries metadata) are skipped at discovery. NvmeIoOp NVMe NVM-command-set IO opcodes (NVM Express 1.4 ┬з6.x). NvmeNamespace One NVMe namespace exposed as an IBlockDevice. Reads and writes are issued one LBA at a time through the parent controller's per-slot bounce buffers тАФ multiple namespaces (or concurrent callers on the same namespace) execute in parallel up to the controller's I/O queue depth minus one (the NVMe queue-full rule). NvmeRegisters NVMe controller register block accessor. Wraps the BAR0 virtual base (BAR0 phys + HHDM offset) and exposes the spec registers via Native.MMIO. Layout follows NVM Express 1.4 ┬з3.1. PortRegisters AHCI Port Registers. Carries a back-reference to the AhciController that owns the port so per-port code (SATA command issue, port reset) can reach controller state without going through globals. Sata SATA drive port driver. The bounce buffer is a single page allocated from PageAllocator; the HBA DMAs to its physical address while ReadDataBlock16(ushort[]) / WriteDataBlock8(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) touch the kernel-virtual address. Error contract: command issue throws on failure (no free slot, device timeout, task-file error), so ReadBlock(ulong, ulong, Span<byte>) / WriteBlock(ulong, ulong, ReadOnlySpan<byte>) never silently hand back stale bounce-buffer contents. All I/O on a port is serialized by an internal lock тАФ the port owns a single bounce buffer. Structs NvmeCqe NVMe Completion Queue Entry (16 bytes). Reads only тАФ the controller writes these. NvmeSqe NVMe Submission Queue Entry (64 bytes). Backed by raw memory; the helpers fill the spec-defined CDWs without using managed structs (so the layout stays exactly as the controller expects regardless of runtime padding). Enums AhciSignature AHCI drive signature to identify what drive is plugged to Port. AtaCommands ATA Commands. AtaDeviceStatus ATA Device Status bits. CommandAndStatus Command and Status register bits. CurrentInterfaceSpeedStatus SATA Status: Current Interface Speed. DeviceDetectionStatus SATA Status: Device Detection Status. FisType FIS (Frame Information Structure) type enumeration. InterfacePowerManagementStatus SATA Status: Interface Power Management Status. InterruptStatus Interrupt Status bits. PortType Port type enumeration.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Vfs.ModeEnum.html.title: Enum ModeEnum | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Vfs.ModeEnum.html.summary: Enum ModeEnum Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Vfs Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.dll POSIX-style file mode flags used by the VFS layer. [Flags] public enum ModeEnum Fields BlockDevice = CharacterDevice | Directory Block device. CharacterDevice = 8192 Character device. Directory = 16384 Directory. FileTypeMask = NamedPipe | CharacterDevice | Socket Mask for file type bits. GroupExecute = 8 Execute permission for group. GroupRead = 32 Read permission for group. GroupWrite = 16 Write permission for group. NamedPipe = 4096 Named pipe. OtherExecute = 1 Execute permission for others. OtherRead = 4 Read permission for others. OtherWrite = 2 Write permission for others. OwnerExecute = 64 Execute permission for owner. OwnerRead = 256 Read permission for owner. OwnerWrite = 128 Write permission for owner. PermissionMask = OtherExecute | OtherWrite | OtherRead | GroupExecute | GroupWrite | GroupRead | OwnerExecute | OwnerWrite | OwnerRead Mask for permission bits (owner/group/other). RegularFile = 32768 Regular file. SetGroupId = 1024 Set group ID on execution. SetUserId = 2048 Set user ID on execution. Socket = Directory | RegularFile Socket. Sticky = 512 Sticky bit. SymbolicLink = CharacterDevice | RegularFile Symbolic link. Remarks Layout mirrors Unix mode bits: Permissions: owner/group/other read, write, execute (bits 0-8) Special: sticky, setgid, setuid (bits 9-11) File type: high nibble encodes the type (bits 12-15); mask with FileTypeMask.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.X64.X64PlatformIni.summary: Class X64PlatformInitializer Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.X64 Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.X64.dll X64 platform initializer - creates x64-specific HAL components. public class X64PlatformInitializer : IPlatformInitializer Inheritance object X64PlatformInitializer Implements IPlatformInitializer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Architecture public PlatformArchitecture Architecture { get; } Property Value PlatformArchitecture PlatformName public string PlatformName { get; } Property Value string Methods CreateCpuOps() public ICpuOps CreateCpuOps() Returns ICpuOps CreateInterruptController() Creates the platform-specific interrupt controller. public IInterruptController CreateInterruptController() Returns IInterruptController CreatePortIO() public IPortIO CreatePortIO() Returns IPortIO CreatePowerOps() public IPowerOps CreatePowerOps() Returns IPowerOps CreateTimer() Creates and initializes the platform timer device. public ITimerDevice CreateTimer() Returns ITimerDevice DelayMicroseconds(uint) Busy-waits for at least microseconds ┬╡s without depending on interrupts or the scheduler тАФ usable from phase-3 device init. x64 paces on legacy port-0x80 reads (~1 ┬╡s each); ARM64 polls the generic timer (CNTVCT/CNTFRQ). public void DelayMicroseconds(uint microseconds) Parameters microseconds uint DmaBarrier() Full data-synchronization barrier ordering prior normal-memory accesses against subsequent device MMIO accesses. DMA drivers call this between filling a descriptor/queue entry in RAM and ringing the device doorbell (and after observing a device-written flag before consuming the data it guards). ARM64 issues dsb sy; x64's total store order already provides this, so it's a no-op. public void DmaBarrier() EnsureMmioMapped(ulong) Maps a physical MMIO region so the HHDM-virtual alias is accessible with Device-memory attributes. Called by HAL device drivers (AHCI, NVMe, etc.) before touching their BARs. ARM64 installs a Device mapping in TTBR1 via DeviceMapper.EnsureMapped; x64's existing page tables already cover MMIO so it's a no-op. public void EnsureMmioMapped(ulong physBase) Parameters physBase ulong Physical base address of the MMIO region. GetCpuCount() Gets the number of CPUs detected on this platform. public uint GetCpuCount() Returns uint GetKeyboardDevices() Gets keyboard devices available on this platform. public IKeyboardDevice[] GetKeyboardDevices() Returns IKeyboardDevice[] GetMouseDevices() Gets mouse devices available on this platform. public IMouseDevice[] GetMouseDevices() Returns IMouseDevice[] GetNetworkDevice() Gets network devices available on this platform. Returns null if no network device found. public INetworkDevice? GetNetworkDevice() Returns INetworkDevice InitializeHardware() Initializes platform-specific hardware (PCI, ACPI, APIC, GIC, etc.). Called after HAL and interrupt manager are initialized. public void InitializeHardware() PreparePciMapping(ulong) Maps PCI configuration space memory before device enumeration. ARM64 maps ECAM as device memory; x64 uses port I/O (no mapping needed). public void PreparePciMapping(ulong ecamBase) Parameters ecamBase ulong Physical ECAM base address from ACPI MCFG. StartSchedulerTimer(uint) Starts the platform timer for preemptive scheduling. Called after all initialization is complete. public void StartSchedulerTimer(uint quantumMs) Parameters quantumMs uint Scheduler time quantum in milliseconds.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.html.summary: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.HAL Namespaces Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.ARM64 Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Cpu.Enums Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Interfaces Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Pci Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Vfs Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.X64 Classes AcpiMcfg C# bridge to native ACPI MCFG discovery (acpi_wrapper.c in MultiArch). The native code is called during early boot (kmain) and parses the MCFG table to extract the PCI ECAM base address. This class just retrieves the result. Native import lives in Cosmos.Kernel.Core/Bridge/Import/AcpiNative.cs. DebugInfo PlatformHAL Platform HAL manager - provides access to platform-specific hardware. Structs AcpiMcfg.McfgInfo Mirrors the C struct acpi_mcfg_info_t from ACPI/acpi_wrapper.c. Must match the native layout exactly.

also the FAT12/16 fixed root, whose storage lies outside the data area. public const uint EmptyFirstCluster = 0 Field Value uint EndOfDirectoryMarker First byte value terminating the directory (no entries follow). public const byte EndOfDirectoryMarker = 0 Field Value byte EntrySize Size in bytes of one 8.3 directory entry (fatgen103 ┬з6). public const int EntrySize = 32 Field Value int FirstClusterHighOffset Byte offset of the high 16 bits of the first cluster (FAT32; reserved on FAT12/16). public const int FirstClusterHighOffset = 20 Field Value int FirstClusterLowOffset Byte offset of the low 16 bits of the first cluster. public const int FirstClusterLowOffset = 26 Field Value int MaxLfnNameLength Longest name the LFN format allows. public const int MaxLfnNameLength = 255 Field Value int SizeFieldBytes Byte width of the 32-bit DIR_FileSize field (fatgen103 ┬з6). public const int SizeFieldBytes = 4 Field Value int SizeOffset Byte offset of the 32-bit file size. public const int SizeOffset = 28 Field Value int Methods BuildShortName(string, Span<char>, ReadOnlySpan<byte>) Build the 8.3 name for longName. When the name does not fit 8.3 as-is, a numeric tail (~1, ~2, ...) is chosen so the result collides with no live short name in directoryData тАФ duplicate short names are invalid on FAT, and short-name lookups would resolve to the wrong file. public static void BuildShortName(string longName, Span<char> dest11, ReadOnlySpan<byte> directoryData) Parameters longName string dest11 Span<char> directoryData ReadOnlySpan<byte> ComputeShortChecksum(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) Checksum over the raw 11 on-disk name bytes (fatgen103 algorithm). public static byte ComputeShortChecksum(ReadOnlySpan<byte> raw11) Parameters raw11 ReadOnlySpan<byte> Returns byte ComputeShortChecksum(ReadOnlySpan<char>) public static byte ComputeShortChecksum(ReadOnlySpan<char> shortName11) Parameters shortName11 ReadOnlySpan<char> Returns byte FindFreeRun(ReadOnlySpan<byte>, int, out bool) Locate the first free entry slot (deleted or unused) where entriesNeeded consecutive 32-byte slots fit. Per spec the 0x00 terminator frees its own slot and everything after it, so scanning stops there and the remaining buffer counts as one free region тАФ stale bytes past the terminator must never reset the run (or be treated as occupied). dest must be writable. public static void WriteShortEntry(Span<byte> dest, int offset, ReadOnlySpan<char> shortName11, FatAttr attributes, uint firstCluster, uint size) Parameters dest Span<byte> offset int shortName11 ReadOnlySpan<char> attributes FatAttr firstCluster uint size uint

Pluggable into the VFS via VfsManager.RegisterFilesystem. Source strings: Empty: use the injected device passed to the constructor (test seam). "<index>": partition index in Partitions. public sealed class FatFilesystemType : IVfsFilesystemType Inheritance object FatFilesystemType Implements IVfsFilesystemType Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors FatFilesystemType() public FatFilesystemType() FatFilesystemType(IBlockDevice) public FatFilesystemType(IBlockDevice device) Parameters device IBlockDevice Methods TryDestroy(ReadOnlySpan<char>) Wipe the filesystem signature on the backing store so it no longer mounts. The underlying device is not zeroed in full. public bool TryDestroy(ReadOnlySpan<char> source) Parameters source ReadOnlySpan<char> Returns bool TryFormat(ReadOnlySpan<char>, IVfsFormatOptions?) Lay down a fresh on-disk filesystem on the backing store identified by source. Drivers cast options to their own concrete IVfsFormatOptions type; passing null selects driver defaults. The source must not be mounted: VfsManager.TryFormat/TryDestroy refuse a source with a live mount, since rewriting the volume underneath a superblock's cached geometry corrupts it. public bool TryFormat(ReadOnlySpan<char> source, IVfsFormatOptions? options) Parameters source ReadOnlySpan<char> options IVfsFormatOptions Returns bool TryMount(ReadOnlySpan<char>, MountFlags, out IVfsSuperblock?) public bool TryMount(ReadOnlySpan<char> source, MountFlags flags, out IVfsSuperblock? superblock) Parameters source ReadOnlySpan<char> Optional backing store identifier (device path, image id, etc.). flags MountFlags Mount flags (MountFlags). superblock IVfsSuperblock Populated superblock on success. api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Filesystems.Fat.summary: Class FatTable Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.System.Filesystems.Fat Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.System.dll FAT12 / FAT16 / FAT32 table accessor and chain manager. All entry reads and writes go through the underlying IBlockDevice using FatBootSector geometry. Cluster numbers coming from on-disk metadata are untrusted: accessors treat anything outside the volume's data clusters as end-of-chain and never let it drive I/O. public sealed class FatTable Inheritance object FatTable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors FatTable(IBlockDevice, FatBootSector) public FatTable(IBlockDevice device, FatBootSector boot) Parameters device IBlockDevice boot FatBootSector Fields Fat32BadCluster FAT32 bad-cluster marker. public const uint Fat32BadCluster = 268435447 Field Value uint Fat32EndOfChain Lowest FAT32 entry value of the end-of-chain band. public const uint Fat32EndOfChain = 268435448 Field Value uint FirstDataCluster First data-cluster number; clusters 0 and 1 are reserved. public const uint FirstDataCluster = 2 Field Value uint FreeCluster Entry value marking a free cluster. public const uint FreeCluster = 0 Field Value uint Methods AllocateChain(uint) Allocate a chain of count contiguous-by-FAT clusters; returns first cluster, or 0 on failure. public uint AllocateChain(uint count) Parameters count uint Returns uint CountFree() public uint CountFree() Returns uint EndOfChainMarker() public uint EndOfChainMarker() Returns uint ExtendChain(uint, uint) Append count clusters to the chain whose tail is lastCluster; returns new tail or 0 on failure. public uint ExtendChain(uint lastCluster, uint count) Parameters lastCluster uint count uint Returns uint FindFree() public uint FindFree() Returns uint Free(uint) Mark every cluster in the chain rooted at firstCluster as free. public void Free(uint firstCluster) Parameters firstCluster uint Get(uint) public uint Get(uint cluster) Parameters cluster uint Returns uint GetChain(uint) Walk the chain starting at firstCluster. api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Graphics.Fonts..summary: Class TrueTypeFont Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.System.Graphics.Fonts Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.System.dll Represents a TrueType font. Unlike the fixed-cell bitmap fonts, glyphs have individual widths and pair kerning, and the font can be rasterized at any pixel size. Data holds the raw bytes of the .ttf file; Width and Height are zero because a TrueType font has no fixed character cell. Rasterized glyphs are cached per (character, size) as grayscale coverage, so the same font instance can draw in any color without re-rasterizing. public class TrueTypeFont : Font Inheritance object Font TrueTypeFont Inherited Members Font.Data Font.Height Font.Width Font.ConvertByteToBitAddress(byte, int) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors TrueTypeFont(byte[], int) Loads a TrueType font from the raw bytes of a .ttf file. public TrueTypeFont(byte[] fontData, int sizePx = 16) Parameters fontData byte[] The raw bytes of the .ttf file. sizePx int The default text size in pixels. api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Graphics.Fonts..summary: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.System.Graphics.Fonts Classes Font Represents a bitmap font. PCScreenFont Represents a font in the PC Screen Font (PCF) format. PCScreenFont.Default TrueTypeFont Represents a TrueType font. Unlike the fixed-cell bitmap fonts, glyphs have individual widths and pair kerning, and the font can be rasterized at any pixel size. Data holds the raw bytes of the .ttf file; Width and Height are zero because a TrueType font has no fixed character cell. Rasterized glyphs are cached per (character, size) as grayscale coverage, so the same font instance can draw in any color without re-rasterizing. Structs UnicodeMapping Represents a Unicode to font position mapping.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Graphics.html.summary: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.System.Graphics Namespaces Cosmos.Kernel.System.Graphics.Fonts Classes Bitmap Represents a bitmap image. FullScreenCanvas Provides functionality to fetch canvases that write directly to the underlying display device. Image Represents a raster image. KernelConsole Cell-based graphics console for kernel output. Uses a character grid (cells) similar to Aura OS for efficient terminal rendering. Can be instantiated on any canvas (hardware or virtual). Png Represents a PNG image. All PNG color types are supported (grayscale, truecolor and palette, with or without alpha), at every legal bit depth, with or without Adam7 interlacing. Decoding is done entirely in managed code by the vendored BigGustave decoder and SharpZipLib inflater (see the Credits page of the documentation). Structs Cell Represents a single cell in the terminal grid. Each cell contains a character and its associated colors. Mode Represents a video mode, definining its width (rows), height (columns) and color depth. Enums ColorDepth Represents the color depth of the pixels of a graphics surface. ColorOrder Describes the order in which the colors are stored in each pixel of an image. ImageFormat Supported image formats.

When this method returns, the buffer contains the specified byte array with the values between offset and (offset + count - 1) replaced by the bytes read from the current source. offset int The zero-based byte offset in buffer at which to begin storing the data read from the current stream. count int The maximum number of bytes to be read from the current stream. This can be less than the size of the buffer if that many bytes are not currently available, or zero (0) if the buffer's length is zero or the end of the stream has been reached. ReadByte() Reads a byte from the stream and advances the position within the stream by one byte, or returns -1 if at the end of the stream. public override int ReadByte() Returns int The unsigned byte cast to an int, or -1 if at the end of the stream. api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Network.IPv4.TC.title: Enum Flags | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Network.IPv4.TC.summary: Enum Flags Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.System.Network.IPv4.TCP Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.System.dll TCP Flags public enum Flags : byte Fields ACK = 16 Acknowledgment field significant. FIN = 1 No more data from sender. PSH = 8 Push Function. RST = 4 Reset the connection. SYN = 2 Synchronize sequence numbers. URG = 32 Urgent Pointer field significant.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Network.IPv4.TC.title: Enum Status | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Network.IPv4.TC.summary: Enum Status Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.System.Network.IPv4.TCP Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.System.dll Represents a TCP connection status. public enum Status Fields CLOSED = 10 Represents no connection state. CLOSE_WAIT = 6 Wait for a connection termination request from the local user. CLOSING = 7 Wait for a connection termination request acknowledgment from the remote TCP. ESTABLISHED = 3 Represents an open connection, data received can be delivered to the user. The normal state for the data transfer phase of the connection. FIN_WAIT1 = 4 Wait for a connection termination request from the remote TCP, or an acknowledgment of the connection termination request previously sent. FIN_WAIT2 = 5 Wait for a connection termination request from the remote TCP. LAST_ACK = 8 Wait for an acknowledgment of the connection termination request previously sent to the remote TCP (which includes an acknowledgment of its connection termination request). LISTEN = 0 Wait for a connection request from any remote TCP and port. SYN_RECEIVED = 2 Wait for a confirming connection request acknowledgment after having both received and sent a connection request. SYN_SENT = 1 Wait for a matching connection request after having sent a connection request. TIME_WAIT = 9 Wait for enough time to pass to be sure the remote TCP received the acknowledgment of its connection termination request.

api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.Ebr.htm.title: Class Ebr | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.Ebr.htm.summary: Class Ebr Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.System.dll EBR (Extended Boot Record) chain parser and editor. An extended partition contains a singly-linked list of EBR sectors; each EBR holds one logical partition entry plus an optional pointer to the next EBR. Convention used: Logical-entry start LBA is relative to the EBR sector that holds it. Next-EBR pointer is relative to the extended partition's start LBA (the standard convention). The first EBR always sits at the extended partition's start LBA. Each subsequent EBR is placed immediately after the prior logical's data. public static class Ebr Inheritance object Ebr Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods AddLogical(IBlockDevice, ulong, ulong, byte, ulong) Append a logical partition to the chain. The new EBR is placed right after the prior logical's data, and the new logical's data area follows the new EBR (one sector for the EBR, then sectorCount sectors). Returns the new logical's absolute start LBA, or 0 on failure. public static ulong AddLogical(IBlockDevice device, ulong extendedStartLba, ulong extendedSectorCount, byte systemId, ulong sectorCount) Parameters device IBlockDevice extendedStartLba ulong extendedSectorCount ulong systemId byte sectorCount ulong Returns ulong MoveLogical(IBlockDevice, ulong, int, ulong) Rewrite the start LBA of the logicalIndex-th logical partition. The hosting EBR sector stays put; only the relative offset inside the EBR changes. Caller is responsible for making sure data at the new range is what's expected (use MoveWithData(IBlockDevice, PartitionLocation, ulong) for a data-copying move). public static bool MoveLogical(IBlockDevice device, ulong extendedStartLba, int logicalIndex, ulong newAbsoluteStart) Parameters device IBlockDevice extendedStartLba ulong logicalIndex int newAbsoluteStart ulong Returns bool Parse(IBlockDevice, ulong) Walk the EBR chain rooted at extendedStartLba and return one entry per logical partition. StartSector values are absolute LBAs on device. public static List<Mbr.PartitionEntry> Parse(IBlockDevice device, ulong extendedStartLba) Parameters device IBlockDevice extendedStartLba ulong Returns List<Mbr.PartitionEntry> RemoveLogical(IBlockDevice, ulong, int) Remove the logicalIndex-th logical partition from the chain (0-based, in chain order). public static bool RemoveLogical(IBlockDevice device, ulong extendedStartLba, int logicalIndex) Parameters device IBlockDevice extendedStartLba ulong logicalIndex int Returns bool ResizeLogical(IBlockDevice, ulong, int, ulong) Rewrite the SectorCount of the logicalIndex-th logical partition. public static bool ResizeLogical(IBlockDevice device, ulong extendedStartLba, int logicalIndex, ulong newSectorCount) Parameters device IBlockDevice extendedStartLba ulong logicalIndex int newSectorCount ulong Returns bool

api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.Gpt.htm.summary: Class Gpt Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.System.dll GPT (GUID Partition Table) parser / writer. Reads / writes the primary header at LBA 1 plus the partition entry array starting at LBA 2. 512-byte sectors assumed (matches every block device this kernel currently exposes). public static class Gpt Inheritance object Gpt Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields BasicDataPartitionType Microsoft Basic Data Partition GUID тАФ used by FAT/NTFS/exFAT volumes. public static readonly Guid BasicDataPartitionType Field Value Guid FirstUsableLba First usable LBA in the standard layout: protective MBR + header + 32 entry-array sectors occupy LBAs 0..33. Public so shell tooling validates against the same value the writer stamps. public const ulong FirstUsableLba = 34 Field Value ulong PrimaryHeaderLba LBA of the primary GPT header (sector 1, UEFI spec). Public so MBR creation and tests wipe the same sector this writer stamps, mirroring FirstUsableLba. public const ulong PrimaryHeaderLba = 1 Field Value ulong ProtectiveMbrStartLba First absolute LBA of the protective partition (LBA 1, right after the MBR; UEFI spec 5.2.3). Public so tests craft protective entries with the same value this writer stamps. public const uint ProtectiveMbrStartLba = 1 Field Value uint Methods AddPartition(IBlockDevice, ulong, ulong, Guid) Add a partition entry of partitionType covering sectorCount sectors starting at startSector. Returns false if the GPT header is missing or no slot is free. public static bool AddPartition(IBlockDevice device, ulong startSector, ulong sectorCount, Guid partitionType) Parameters device IBlockDevice startSector ulong sectorCount ulong partitionType Guid Returns bool Create(IBlockDevice) Write a fresh GPT layout: protective MBR at LBA 0, primary header at LBA 1, and 32 zeroed sectors of 128-byte partition entries (LBAs 2..33). Backup header / array intentionally not written тАФ callers targeting test images don't need them, and writing them needs CRC support that the boot path doesn't yet ship. public static void Create(IBlockDevice device) Parameters device IBlockDevice IsGpt(IBlockDevice) True if the GPT header at LBA 1 starts with the EFI PART signature. public static bool IsGpt(IBlockDevice device) Parameters device IBlockDevice Returns bool MovePartition(IBlockDevice, int, ulong) Rewrite the start and end LBAs of partitionIndex so the partition lives at newStartSector with the same length. Table-level only тАФ does not relocate data. public static bool MovePartition(IBlockDevice device, int partitionIndex, ulong newStartSector) Parameters device IBlockDevice partitionIndex int newStartSector ulong Returns bool Parse(IBlockDevice) Walk the GPT partition entry array. Empty slots (zero PartitionType GUID) are skipped. public static List<Gpt.PartitionEntry> Parse(IBlockDevice device) Parameters device IBlockDevice Returns List<Gpt.PartitionEntry> RemovePartition(IBlockDevice, int) Mark the partitionIndex-th non-empty entry as deleted by zeroing the whole entry тАФ UEFI expects unused entries fully zeroed, and a stale UTF-16 name would resurface when AddPartition(IBlockDevice, ulong, ulong, Guid) reuses the slot without rewriting it. public static bool RemovePartition(IBlockDevice device, int partitionIndex) Parameters device IBlockDevice partitionIndex int Returns bool ResizePartition(IBlockDevice, int, ulong) Rewrite the end LBA of partitionIndex so the partition spans newSectorCount sectors. api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.html.title: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage | Cosmos Gen 3
api/Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.html.summary: Namespace Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage Classes Ebr EBR (Extended Boot Record) chain parser and editor. An extended partition contains a singly-linked list of EBR sectors; each EBR holds one logical partition entry plus an optional pointer to the next EBR. Convention used: Logical-entry start LBA is relative to the EBR sector that holds it. Next-EBR pointer is relative to the extended partition's start LBA (the standard convention). The first EBR always sits at the extended partition's start LBA. Each subsequent EBR is placed immediately after the prior logical's data. Gpt GPT (GUID Partition Table) parser / writer. Reads / writes the primary header at LBA 1 plus the partition entry array starting at LBA 2. 512-byte sectors assumed (matches every block device this kernel currently exposes). Gpt.PartitionEntry Single parsed partition. Sector positions are absolute on the host disk. Mbr MBR (Master Boot Record) partition table parser / writer. Operates on any IBlockDevice with 512-byte sectors. Mbr.PartitionEntry Single MBR partition entry. Sector positions are absolute on the host disk. Partition Block-device view of a single partition on a host disk. Read/Write are translated to the host LBA space by adding StartSector. PartitionManager High-level partition lifecycle (create / delete / resize / move) on top of Mbr and Gpt. Auto-detects the table type per call. MoveWithData(IBlockDevice, PartitionLocation, ulong) physically copies sectors before rewriting the table; the other operations are pure table edits. StorageManager Manages block storage devices. Structs PartitionManager.PartitionLocation Identifies a partition by its absolute LBA range on the host disk.

api/ICSharpCode.SharpZipLib.Checksum.Adl.title: Class Adler32 | Cosmos Gen 3
api/ICSharpCode.SharpZipLib.Checksum.Adl.summary: Class Adler32 Namespace ICSharpCode.SharpZipLib.Checksum Assembly Cosmos.Kernel.System.dll Computes Adler32 checksum for a stream of data. An Adler32 checksum is not as reliable as a CRC32 checksum, but a lot faster to compute. The specification for Adler32 may be found in RFC 1950. ZLIB Compressed Data Format Specification version 3.3) From that document: "ADLER32 (Adler-32 checksum) This contains a checksum value of the uncompressed data (excluding any dictionary data) computed according to Adler-32 algorithm. This algorithm is a 32-bit extension and improvement of the Fletcher algorithm, used in the ITU-T X.224 / ISO 8073 standard. Adler-32 is composed of two sums accumulated per byte: s1 is the sum of all bytes, s2 is the sum of all s1 values. Both sums are done modulo 65521. s1 is initialized to 1, s2 to zero. The Adler-32 checksum is stored as s2*65536 + s1 in most- significant-byte first (network) order." "8.2. The Adler-32 algorithm The Adler-32 algorithm is much faster than the CRC32 algorithm yet still provides an extremely low probability of undetected errors. The modulo on unsigned long accumulators can be delayed for 5552 bytes, so the modulo operation time is negligible. If the bytes are a, b, c, the second sum is 3a + 2b + c + 3, and so is position and order sensitive, unlike the first sum, which is just a checksum. That 65521 is prime is important to avoid a possible large class of two-byte errors that leave the check unchanged. (The Fletcher checksum uses 255, which is not prime and which also makes the Fletcher check insensitive to single byte changes 0 - 255.) The sum s1 is initialized to 1 instead of zero to make the length of the sequence part of s2, so that the length does not have to be checked separately. (Any sequence of zeroes has a Fletcher checksum of zero.)" public sealed class Adler32 : IChecksum Inheritance object Adler32 Implements IChecksum Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors Adler32() Initialise a default instance of Adler32 public Adler32() Properties Value Returns the Adler32 data checksum computed so far. public long Value { get; } Property Value long Methods Reset() Resets the Adler32 data checksum as if no update was ever called. public void Reset() Update(ArraySegment<byte>) Update Adler32 data checksum based on a portion of a block of data public void Update(ArraySegment<byte> segment) Parameters segment ArraySegment<byte> The chunk of data to add Update(byte[]) Updates the Adler32 data checksum with the bytes taken from a block of data. public void Update(byte[] buffer) Parameters buffer byte[] Contains the data to update the checksum with. Update(int) Updates the checksum with the byte b. public void Update(int bval) Parameters bval int The data value to add. The high byte of the int is ignored.

This algorithm is a 32-bit extension and improvement of the Fletcher algorithm, used in the ITU-T X.224 / ISO 8073 standard. Adler-32 is composed of two sums accumulated per byte: s1 is the sum of all bytes, s2 is the sum of all s1 values. Both sums are done modulo 65521. s1 is initialized to 1, s2 to zero. The Adler-32 checksum is stored as s2*65536 + s1 in most- significant-byte first (network) order." "8.2. The Adler-32 algorithm The Adler-32 algorithm is much faster than the CRC32 algorithm yet still provides an extremely low probability of undetected errors. The modulo on unsigned long accumulators can be delayed for 5552 bytes, so the modulo operation time is negligible. If the bytes are a, b, c, the second sum is 3a + 2b + c + 3, and so is position and order sensitive, unlike the first sum, which is just a checksum. That 65521 is prime is important to avoid a possible large class of two-byte errors that leave the check unchanged. (The Fletcher checksum uses 255, which is not prime and which also makes the Fletcher check insensitive to single byte changes 0 - 255.) The sum s1 is initialized to 1 instead of zero to make the length of the sequence part of s2, so that the length does not have to be checked separately. (Any sequence of zeroes has a Fletcher checksum of zero.)" Interfaces IChecksum Interface to compute a data checksum used by checked input/output streams. A data checksum can be updated by one byte or with a byte array. After each update the value of the current checksum can be returned by calling 
getValue
. The complete checksum object can also be reset so it can be used again with new data.
